reasoning: The question resolves YES if NFL approves a rule restricting the Tush Push before June 1, 2025. Current evidence strongly suggests this will happen: (1) CONFIRMED TIMING: May 20-21 meeting is 10 days before the June 1 deadline (search_1_result_0, search_1_result_1), (2) EXPANDED SCOPE: The proposal shifted from targeting only Tush Push to banning ALL pushing/pulling ball carriers, which may broaden support (search_1_result_2, search_0_summary), (3) STRENGTHENING MOMENTUM: Early April showed 16-16 split (search_3_result_1, search_2_result_4), but by May 4 Washington Post reported 'strong support expected' for expanded version (search_1_result_2), and May 12 Northeast Times stated 24 teams expected to vote yes - exactly the threshold (lookup_0, search_2_result_3), (4) OFFICIAL SUPPORT: Commissioner Goodell and Competition Committee support the change (search_0_summary, search_2_summary), (5) NO INJURY DATA: While no injury data supports ban (search_2_summary), the 'image of the game' argument appears to be winning. The probability balances the strong late momentum (68%) against some uncertainty about whether the expectation will materialize (32% chance it falls short of 24 votes or is delayed). The May 20-21 date provides adequate buffer from June 1 deadline.
